threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still carry an umbrella everywhere; we know you haven’t learned yet that you’ll just end up soaked anyway.
Last night on the 35, a bloke dropped a whole kebab on the floor and just kept staring at it like it was a life decision. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the old man selling handmade candles next to the döner kiosk, chatting with the hipsters and locals alike. That blend is Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg have I seen a kebab shop that opens at 3 AM and serves vegan döner. A true late-night lifesaver for the post-club crowd.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively check BART’s status before heading out, but still end up waiting while a dog disrupts Muni.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo waiting for a Muni bus last night, clutching a bouquet. Maybe he was late to a wedding or just celebrating the weekend. Classic SF!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars close early. But locals know the best ones, like La Madre, don’t really get going until after midnight.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their route to the nearest metro; they’ll always hesitate at the ticket machines and fumble with their change.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the bus stop instead of just checking your phone. A classic rookie mistake.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the entire bus with his guitar. Surprisingly decent, but the bus driver seemed more embarrassed than us.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street musician playing Bach on a saxophone while a group of kids were breakdancing nearby. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a club inside a converted public toilet. The graffiti art alone is worth the trip.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center during rush hour, especially with BART in chaos. It's a wild ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo juggling bags of takeout on the Muni yesterday while giving a TED talk to an invisible audience. Only in 'Frisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the quiet charms of our local plazas, where real life unfolds without the snap of a camera.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still say 'Adéu' instead of 'Adiós' when leaving a bar—locals switched to the Spanish version years ago.
